Cross-sectional analysis: Graduates of each cohort are observed two years after graduation (for example, graduates of calendar year 2010 are observed in 2012). The criteria used to define the graduate subpopulations shown under Characteristics after graduation are only applied in the year of the earnings measurement. See the technical reference guide <a href=https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/pub/37-20-0001/372000012025001-eng.htm" rel="external noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Labour market outcome indicators of postsecondary graduates
column3: 2010 to 2021 (opens new window) for further information."

column2: The data are subject to annual revision. The year chosen for constant dollars may also be updated.
Refers to the age of graduates on December 31st of the year of graduation. The study population only includes graduates between 15 and 64 years old.

Status of student in Canada is defined on December 31st of the year of graduation. Canadian students" include Canadian citizens and permanent residents. "International students" include students studying in Canada on student visas

column2: Estimates may not be available for all reference periods for all geographies, due to data limitations. The Canada estimates exclude college graduates for the following provinces and territories and graduate cohorts: Ontario (2010 to 2013), New Brunswick (2010), Manitoba (2010), Territories (2010 and 2011).
Graduates reporting employment income" excludes graduates with no income information and those who were full-time students in the year in which earnings were measured. Graduates reporting $0 in wages
column3: salaries and commissions and in net self-employment income are included in the "Number of graduates" but not in the "Median employment income" estimates. Employment income includes wages
column8: tax exempted Indian employment income) and net self-employment income (net income from business
column11: fishing and commissions). Net self-employment income may be positive

column2: Graduates reporting $0 in wages, salaries and commissions and in net self-employment income are included in the calculation of the Number of graduates"; however
column3: they are excluded from the "Median employment income" calculation. The suppression of the median employment income is based on counts that exclude graduates reporting $0 in wages
column4: salaries and commissions and in net self-employment income. Median employment income is suppressed to protect confidentiality when the count of graduates used to produce that estimate is small."

column2: The Number of graduates" represents the number of individuals. Due to different study population definitions and methodology
column3: graduate counts in this table differ from the published annual counts from the Postsecondary Student Information System (PSIS). All counts are randomly rounded. Due to rounding
column4: components may not add to the totals."

column2: The Median employment income" is not provided for the aggregate "Total
column3: educational qualification".  All dollar figures are rounded to the nearest hundred. Note that income after graduation does not control for the effect of additional education that may have been obtained since the graduation year."

column2: Educational qualification is derived by combining two Postsecondary Student Information System (PSIS) variables: program type and credential type. For consistency and homogeneity across provinces and territories, two adjustments are made. (1) Medicine, Dentistry, Veterinary medicine, Optometry, Pharmacy and Law degrees reported as Undergraduate degrees" or "Post-baccalaureate non-graduate degrees" are regrouped as "Professional degrees." (2) "Post-baccalaureate non-graduate degrees" in the fields of Education and Social work are regrouped with "Undergraduate degrees". <a href="http://www23.statcan.gc.ca/imdb/p3VD.pl?Function=getVD&TVD=1252576" rel="external noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Classification of programs and credentials - professional degree variant (opens new window)<a/>."
Includes Undergraduate degree" and "Post-baccalaureate non-graduate degree" programs in the following fields of study (based on CIP Canada 2016
This variable uses the <a href=https://www23.statcan.gc.ca/imdb/p3VD.pl?Function=getVD&TVD=1486451" rel="external noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"> Science
column8: social science and education (BHASE) groupings (opens new window) variant of the Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) Canada 2021 Version 1.0 with detail on the underlying two-digit and three-digit series. This variable classifies fields of study into STEM (science
column14: social science and education) categories. BHASE also includes fields in legal studies

column2: The STEM category includes fields of study in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
The BHASE category includes fields of study in business, humanities, health, arts, social science, education, legal studies, trades, services, natural resources and conservation.
